title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the acceleration as a function of time, we need to differentiate the position function twice with respect to time.

title: First Derivative: Velocity
text: The velocity function is the first derivative of the position function with respect to time. Differentiate each component of the position vector.

title: Calculate the Velocity Function
text: Differentiate the position function: $$r(t)=3t^2 i-7t^3 j-5t^{\left(-2\right)} k$$. What is the velocity function?

title: Second Derivative: Acceleration
text: The acceleration function is the derivative of the velocity function with respect to time. Differentiate each component of the velocity vector.

title: Calculate the Acceleration Function
text: Differentiate the velocity function: $$v(t)=6t i-21t^2 j+10t^{\left(-3\right)} k$$. What is the acceleration function?
